title: poa glauca subsp. bryophila
intro: Poa glauca subsp. bryophila, also known as blue fescue grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to the Andes mountains in South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Poa glauca subsp. bryophila include the potential treatment of various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic. Poa glauca subsp. bryophila can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
